text.title: Go Error Handling (AppError + Centralised Mapper) Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Go Error Handling (AppError + Centralised Mapper) application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Project MUST define one `apperror.AppError` struct {Code, Message, HTTPStatus, Cause, Stack}; never multiple.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal Go review patterns. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Errors crossing layers MUST be wrapped with `fmt.Errorf("verb: %w", err)` to preserve the chain.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go 1.13 error wrapping. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Each `internal/<feature>` package MUST expose sentinel vars (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`).
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go style guide. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Storage-layer sentinels (`sql.ErrNoRows`) MUST be translated to domain sentinels at the repository boundary.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Errors MUST be logged exactly once — at the outermost handler — not at every layer.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Field guides. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: ONE middleware MUST render `AppError` to the RFC 7807 envelope; handlers return errors, never write JSON.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Hexagonal architecture. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: `fmt.Errorf("...%s", err)` is FORBIDDEN — always `%w` to preserve chain.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go vet checks. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
